Popular Records

Popular urban legends, folklore, and mystery stories

A curated path through reader-friendly entry points for urban legends, internet folklore, classic myths, strange places, and recurring mystery motifs.

Lost Worlds

Agartha Hollow Earth Legend: Hidden Realm, Inner World, and the Geography Beneath the Map

Agartha, also spelled Agharta in some sources, is a hidden-world legend often connected with hollow-earth and esoteric geography. It is imagined as an inner realm, secret civilization, or spiritual center beneath ordinary maps.

Lost Worlds - Hidden World - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Mythic Creatures

Basilisk Folklore: Deadly Gaze, Serpent King, and the Creature No One Should Meet

The basilisk is a mythic creature often described as a deadly serpent or serpent-like king whose gaze, breath, or touch can kill. Medieval and later traditions sometimes blend it with rooster-serpent imagery related to the cockatrice.

Mythic Creatures - Mythic Reptile - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Myths

Phaethon Sun Chariot Myth: Fire, Fall, and the Danger of Driving the Sun

Phaethon is a Greek mythic figure who asks to drive the chariot of the sun and loses control. The burning path threatens the world until Zeus strikes him down.

Myths - Greek Myth - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Modern Legends

Beast of Bray Road: Wisconsin Sightings, Werewolf Shape, and a Modern Monster Legend

The Beast of Bray Road is a modern Wisconsin legend about a wolf-like or werewolf-like creature reported near Bray Road and the Elkhorn area. Witnesses describe a large animal or upright figure with canine features.

Modern Legends - Modern Cryptid - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Classic Folklore

Green Children of Woolpit: Medieval Wonder, Strange Arrival, and the Folklore of Two Children

The Green Children of Woolpit is a medieval English story about two children with green skin who appeared near Woolpit, spoke an unknown language, and at first would eat only beans.

Classic Folklore - Medieval Wonder - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Unexplained Mysteries

Taos Hum Mystery: Low Sound, Witness Reports, and the Noise No One Fully Explains

The Taos Hum is a reported low sound heard by some people around Taos, New Mexico. Witnesses describe a persistent hum, drone, or vibration that not everyone can hear.

Unexplained Mysteries - Unexplained Sound - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Strange Places

Bhangarh Fort Legend: Ruins, Warnings, and the Haunted Place Story of Rajasthan

Bhangarh Fort is a ruined fort in Rajasthan widely associated with haunted-place legends. Retellings connect the site with curses, forbidden nighttime entry, and stories of a settlement that became abandoned.

Strange Places - Haunted Fort - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Internet Folklore

This Man Dream Hoax: The Face, the Website, and the Internet Legend of Shared Dreams

This Man is an internet legend built around a composite-looking face and the claim that many unrelated people had seen him in dreams. The story spread through posters, websites, forums, and image sharing.

Internet Folklore - Internet Hoax - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Urban Legends

Clown Statue Urban Legend: Babysitter, Unwanted Object, and the Figure in the Room

The Clown Statue is a modern urban legend about a babysitter who notices a clown statue or doll in the house, only to learn that the family does not own one. The object becomes frightening because it has been present in the room while being treated as harmless.

Urban Legends - Babysitter Legend - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Urban Legends

Vanishing Hitchhiker Urban Legend: The Passenger Who Disappears Before Arrival

The Vanishing Hitchhiker is a widely known urban legend about a driver who picks up a quiet passenger on a lonely road. The passenger disappears before the ride ends, or the driver later learns that the person died years earlier.

Urban Legends - Roadside Urban Legend - 8 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Internet Folklore

Polybius Arcade Game Legend: The Missing Cabinet, Rumor, and Internet Folklore

Polybius is an internet folklore story about a mysterious arcade game supposedly tested in the early 1980s. The legend says the cabinet caused strange physical or psychological effects, then disappeared.

Internet Folklore - Arcade Game Legend - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Strange Places

Bennington Triangle: Disappearances, Mountain Roads, and a Vermont Place Legend

The Bennington Triangle is a regional place legend connected with disappearances around Glastenbury Mountain and the Bennington area of Vermont. The name groups several events into one mysterious landscape.

Strange Places - Place Legend - 8 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
